req.title: Hr-Admin views expense-claim
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to view a expense-claim through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-044 requires that an authenticated hr-admin can view an expense-claim via the UI, mandatory fields are validated, and an audit record is written on success. The error pathways cover 400 (validation), 401 (unauthenticated), and 403 (forbidden role). I designed: (1) a positive EP test confirming a fully valid hr-admin session renders the expense-claim and triggers an audit entry; (2) a NEG test verifying 401 is returned when the session is unauthenticated; (3) a NEG/DT test verifying 403 when the authenticated user lacks hr-admin role; (4) a BVA test exercising the boundary of the expense-claim ID field (minimum-length valid ID vs empty/zero); (5) a STC test walking the state from 'unauthenticated' through 'authenticated' to 'viewing claim' to verify the full happy-path state chain including audit write. Placeholders (<fixture>, <helper_fn>) are used for shared infrastructure to stay within line limits.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_views_valid_expense_claim
preconditions: At least one expense-claim record exists in the system
actions: Obtain a valid hr-admin session token via <fixture: hr_admin_session>
Navigate to /expense-claims/{valid_claim_id} using Playwright page object
Assert HTTP response status is 200
Assert expense-claim form fields are rendered and populated
Query audit log via <helper_fn: get_latest_audit_record> for the claim view event
expected: Page title or heading contains 'Expense Claim'
All mandatory fields (amount, submitter, date, status) are visible and non-empty
Audit record exists with action='VIEW', entity_type='expense-claim', and matching entity_id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_hr_admin_views_valid_expense_claim(hr_admin_page, valid_claim_id, get_latest_audit_record):
    page = hr_admin_page
    response = page.goto(f"/expense-claims/{valid_claim_id}")
    assert response.status == 200

    expect(page.locator("h1, [data-testid='claim-title']")).to_contain_text("Expense Claim")

    for field in ["amount", "submitter", "date", "status"]:
        locator = page.locator(f"[data-testid='field-{field}']")
        expect(locator).to_be_visible()
        assert locator.inner_text().strip() != ""

    audit = get_latest_audit_record(entity_type="expense-claim", entity_id=valid_claim_id, action="VIEW")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record not written for expense-claim view"
    assert audit["entity_id"] == str(valid_claim_id)

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_expense_claim
preconditions: No active session cookie or auth token is present
actions: Clear all browser cookies and storage via <fixture: anonymous_page>
Attempt to navigate to /expense-claims/{valid_claim_id}
Capture the resulting HTTP status or redirect
expected: Response status is 401 OR user is redirected to /login
Expense-claim form fields are NOT rendered
No audit record is written for this attempt
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_expense_claim(anonymous_page, valid_claim_id, get_latest_audit_record):
    page = anonymous_page
    response = page.goto(f"/expense-claims/{valid_claim_id}")

    is_401 = response.status == 401
    is_redirected_to_login = "/login" in page.url
    assert is_401 or is_redirected_to_login, (
        f"Expected 401 or redirect to /login, got status={response.status} url={page.url}"
    )

    form = page.locator("[data-testid='expense-claim-form']")
    expect(form).not_to_be_visible()

    audit = get_latest_audit_record(entity_type="expense-claim", entity_id=valid_claim_id, action="VIEW")
    assert audit is None, "Audit record must NOT be written for unauthenticated access"

tests.name: test_non_hr_admin_role_forbidden_from_expense_claim
preconditions: User is authenticated but holds role 'employee' (not hr-admin)
actions: Obtain a session for a non-hr-admin user via <fixture: employee_session_page>
Navigate to /expense-claims/{valid_claim_id}
Capture HTTP status and page content
expected: Response status is 403 OR a 'Forbidden' / 'Access Denied' message is displayed
Expense-claim form fields are NOT rendered
No audit record is written for this attempt
trace: R-WEB-044: error pathway 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_non_hr_admin_role_forbidden_from_expense_claim(employee_session_page, valid_claim_id, get_latest_audit_record):
    page = employee_session_page
    response = page.goto(f"/expense-claims/{valid_claim_id}")

    is_403 = response.status == 403
    has_forbidden_text = page.locator("text=/Forbidden|Access Denied|Not Authorized/i").count() > 0
    assert is_403 or has_forbidden_text, (
        f"Expected 403 or forbidden message, got status={response.status}"
    )

    form = page.locator("[data-testid='expense-claim-form']")
    expect(form).not_to_be_visible()

    audit = get_latest_audit_record(entity_type="expense-claim", entity_id=valid_claim_id, action="VIEW")
    assert audit is None, "Audit record must NOT be written for forbidden role access"

tests.name: test_expense_claim_view_with_boundary_claim_id
preconditions: Minimum-valid claim ID (e.g. integer 1 or single-char string) exists; ID 0 or empty does not exist
actions: Obtain hr-admin session via <fixture: hr_admin_page>
Navigate to /expense-claims/1 (minimum boundary ID)
Assert success response and rendered form
Navigate to /expense-claims/0 (below-minimum boundary ID)
Assert 400 or 404 error response
expected: GET /expense-claims/1 returns 200 and renders expense-claim form
GET /expense-claims/0 returns 400 or 404 and does NOT render form
Audit record written only for the valid (ID=1) request
trace: R-WEB-044: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-044: error pathway 400 on validation error
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_expense_claim_view_with_boundary_claim_id(hr_admin_page, get_latest_audit_record):
    page = hr_admin_page

    # --- Minimum valid boundary: ID = 1 ---
    resp_valid = page.goto("/expense-claims/1")
    assert resp_valid.status == 200, f"Expected 200 for ID=1, got {resp_valid.status}"
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='expense-claim-form']")).to_be_visible()
    audit = get_latest_audit_record(entity_type="expense-claim", entity_id="1", action="VIEW")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must exist for valid boundary ID=1"

    # --- Below-minimum boundary: ID = 0 ---
    resp_invalid = page.goto("/expense-claims/0")
    assert resp_invalid.status in (400, 404), (
        f"Expected 400 or 404 for ID=0, got {resp_invalid.status}"
    )
    form = page.locator("[data-testid='expense-claim-form']")
    expect(form).not_to_be_visible()
    audit_zero = get_latest_audit_record(entity_type="expense-claim", entity_id="0", action="VIEW")
    assert audit_zero is None, "Audit record must NOT be written for invalid boundary ID=0"

tests.name: test_expense_claim_view_state_transition_unauth_to_auth_to_viewing
actions: Start with anonymous_page (state: UNAUTHENTICATED)
Attempt /expense-claims/{id} — expect redirect to /login (state: UNAUTHENTICATED -> REDIRECT)
Submit valid hr-admin credentials on login form (state: AUTHENTICATING)
Assert redirect back to /expense-claims/{id} after login (state: AUTHENTICATED)
Assert expense-claim form is rendered with status 200 (state: VIEWING)
Assert audit record written (state: AUDIT_WRITTEN)
expected: After login, user lands on /expense-claims/{id} with status 200
Expense-claim form fields are visible and populated
Audit record written with action=VIEW and matching entity_id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_expense_claim_view_state_transition_unauth_to_auth_to_viewing(
    anonymous_page, valid_claim_id, hr_admin_credentials, get_latest_audit_record
):
    page = anonymous_page

    # State: UNAUTHENTICATED -> navigating to protected route
    page.goto(f"/expense-claims/{valid_claim_id}")
    assert "/login" in page.url, "Expected redirect to /login for unauthenticated user"

    # State: AUTHENTICATING -> submit credentials
    page.fill("[data-testid='username']", hr_admin_credentials["username"])
    page.fill("[data-testid='password']", hr_admin_credentials["password"])
    with page.expect_navigation():
        page.click("[data-testid='login-submit']")

    # State: AUTHENTICATED -> should land back on claim page
    assert f"/expense-claims/{valid_claim_id}" in page.url, (
        f"Expected redirect back to claim, got {page.url}"
    )
    assert page.evaluate("() => window.__httpStatus || 200") == 200

    # State: VIEWING -> form rendered
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='expense-claim-form']")).to_be_visible()

    # State: AUDIT_WRITTEN -> verify audit
    audit = get_latest_audit_record(entity_type="expense-claim", entity_id=str(valid_claim_id), action="VIEW")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must be written after successful state transition to VIEWING"
